seoTitle: Grant Accountant Access to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Invite your accountant or bookkeeping firm into QuickBooks Online securely without sharing your master login credentials.
intro: Getting your accountant into your books shouldn't mean handing over your login. This guide shows how to use QuickBooks Online's dedicated accounting firm invite feature to grant secure external access for tax season or ongoing bookkeeping review.

steps.heading: Open user settings
steps.body: Click the gear icon in the top right and select Manage users under the Your Company column.

steps.heading: Go to the Accounting Firms tab
steps.body: Switch to the Accounting firms tab in the top navigation ribbon of the Manage Users page.

steps.heading: Enter their email
steps.body: Type your accountant's professional business email address into the invite field.

steps.heading: Send the invite
steps.body: Click the Invite button to trigger a secure handshake request, which will show as Pending until accepted.

steps.heading: Confirm and manage access
steps.body: Once your accountant accepts from their own inbox, the row updates to an active linked state; use the Action dropdown later to modify or revoke access.

faq.question: Do I need to share my password with my accountant?
faq.answer: No, the Accounting Firms invite feature grants access without ever sharing your master login credentials.

faq.question: How will I know if my accountant accepted the invite?
faq.answer: The firm's row in Manage Users will shift automatically from Pending to an active linked status once they accept.

faq.question: Can I revoke accountant access later?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Action column dropdown next to the firm's row to modify or remove their access at any time.

faq.question: What kind of access does an accountant get?
faq.answer: Accounting firms typically receive elevated administrative access suited for reviewing and adjusting your books, distinct from standard staff user roles.
